Object.Name: AppMonitoring
Object.Description1:
This LwM2M Object provides app-scoped runtime monitoring and health visibility.
Resource identifiers are intentionally grouped with gaps between logical sections to allow future GEISA-defined resources to be added near related identity, metadata, lifecycle, health, watchdog, resource-use, platform-action, and monitoring resources without renumbering existing resources. Undefined resource identifiers in those gaps are not implemented resources.
Each Object Instance represents the platform view of one installed or running application instance. This Object provides application operational health, lifecycle visibility, resource consumption, restart behavior, watchdog state, and platform actions.
Accounting, quota, throttle, block, disabled, and accounting-window state are represented by linked /3602 AppAccounting instances, not duplicated in /3604.

Item.Name: Application Package ID
Item.Description: Reverse-DNS or package-style application identifier from the application manifest or platform catalog. This value identifies the application package for operator display, logs, and correlation. It is not a unique runtime application instance identifier.

Item.Name: Application Instance ID
Item.Description: Platform-local application instance identifier assigned by the platform or registry. This value distinguishes multiple deployed or running instances of the same package on the same platform. It is not globally unique by itself; upstream correlation requires endpoint or platform identity and, where needed, AppID or application-instance context.

Item.Name: Software Instance
Item.Description: Optional object link to the corresponding /9 Software Management instance for the installed application package or package lifecycle correlation. This link provides package-management context for the monitored application. Resource 4050 AppID provides the local application identifier used by GEISA application monitoring. End-to-end uniqueness requires the EMS to interpret these identifiers in the context of the LwM2M endpoint, platform identity, and available Software Management package metadata.

Item.Name: App Accounting Instances
Item.Description: One or more object links to /3602 AppAccounting instance(s) associated with this application or application instance. Multiple /3602 instances may exist for different accounting classes, scopes, or accounting windows such as daily, since-reboot, rolling-window, lifetime, or operator-defined accounting. /3602 owns accounting counters, quota, throttle, block, disabled, and accounting-window state.

Item.Name: AppID
Item.Description: A unique local-scope identifier for distinguishing edge applications within a specific deployment environment.

Item.Name: Application Name
Item.Description: Human-readable application name from the application manifest or platform application catalog.

Item.Name: Application Version
Item.Description: Application version from the application manifest or platform application catalog.

Item.Name: Execution Environment
Item.Description:
Execution environment used by this application instance.
0 = Unknown 1 = Linux Execution Environment 2 = Virtual Execution Environment 3 = Other or future GEISA execution environment 4..127 = Reserved for future GEISA-defined execution environments 128..255 = Reserved for vendor-specific execution environments

Item.Name: Lifecycle State
Item.Description:
Platform lifecycle state for this application instance.
0 = Unknown 1 = Installed 2 = Activated 3 = Starting 4 = Running 5 = Stopping 6 = Stopped 7 = Failed 8 = Quarantined or policy blocked 9..127 = Reserved for future GEISA-defined lifecycle states 128..255 = Reserved for vendor-specific or operator-specific lifecycle states
This state is the platform's operational view and does not replace Software Management state.

Item.Name: Health State
Item.Description:
Current health summary for this application instance.
0 = Unknown 1 = Healthy 2 = Degraded 3 = Failed 4 = Stopped 5 = Policy blocked 6 = Not reporting 7..127 = Reserved for future GEISA-defined health states 128..255 = Reserved for vendor-specific or operator-specific health states
Health State is a summary. Detailed events, alarms, or log records SHOULD be reported through the GEISA message, event, alarm, or log mechanisms.

Item.Name: Health Reason
Item.Description: Short platform-generated reason associated with the current Health State.

Item.Name: Start Time
Item.Description: Time at which the current or most recent application runtime execution started, when available from the platform. The value is encoded as LwM2M Time using Unix epoch seconds. The encoded value does not carry timezone metadata. Platform-local interpretation uses /3/0/15 Timezone, with fallback to /3/0/14 UTC Offset when Timezone is unavailable, unless a more specific ADM or EMS policy applies.

Item.Name: Last Status Time
Item.Description: Time at which the platform most recently received, generated, or refreshed application runtime status for this application instance. The value is encoded as LwM2M Time using Unix epoch seconds. The encoded value does not carry timezone metadata. Platform-local interpretation uses /3/0/15 Timezone, with fallback to /3/0/14 UTC Offset when Timezone is unavailable, unless a more specific ADM or EMS policy applies.

Item.Name: Last Exit Time
Item.Description: Time at which the application most recently exited or was terminated. The value is encoded as LwM2M Time using Unix epoch seconds. The encoded value does not carry timezone metadata. Platform-local interpretation uses /3/0/15 Timezone, with fallback to /3/0/14 UTC Offset when Timezone is unavailable, unless a more specific ADM or EMS policy applies.

Item.Name: Last Exit Reason
Item.Description:
Reason for the most recent application exit.
0 = None or not applicable 1 = Unknown 2 = Normal exit 3 = App-requested restart 4 = App-requested stop 5 = Platform-requested stop 6 = Watchdog timeout 7 = Crash or abnormal exit 8 = Resource limit exceeded 9 = Policy or security violation 10..127 = Reserved for future GEISA-defined exit reasons 128..255 = Reserved for vendor-specific or operator-specific exit reasons

Item.Name: Restart Count Current Period
Item.Description: Number of application restarts during the current platform-defined monitoring period.

Item.Name: Restart Count Lifetime
Item.Description: Number of application restarts observed across the lifetime of this application installation, subject to platform persistence policy.

Item.Name: Crash Count Current Period
Item.Description: Number of abnormal application exits during the current platform-defined monitoring period.

Item.Name: Watchdog Enabled
Item.Description: Indicates whether the platform is applying an application keepalive or watchdog policy to this application instance.

Item.Name: Watchdog Timeout
Item.Description: Current watchdog timeout in seconds for this application instance.

Item.Name: Watchdog Missed Count
Item.Description: Number of missed application watchdog or keepalive intervals during the current platform-defined monitoring period.

Item.Name: CPU Current Percent
Item.Description: Current or recent CPU utilization estimate for this application instance, reported as a percentage of the CPU allocation or platform basis defined by the platform. This is a bounded estimate, not a perfect platform-wide truth.

Item.Name: CPU Average Percent
Item.Description: Average CPU utilization for this application instance over the platform-defined monitoring window.

Item.Name: CPU Peak Percent
Item.Description: Peak CPU utilization for this application instance over the platform-defined monitoring window.

Item.Name: Memory Current
Item.Description: Current memory used by this application instance, such as resident memory of the representative runtime process when that is the platform measurement basis.

Item.Name: Memory Peak
Item.Description: Peak memory used by this application instance over the platform-defined monitoring window.

Item.Name: Memory Limit
Item.Description: Memory limit currently enforced for this application instance.

Item.Name: Persistent Storage Used
Item.Description: Persistent storage currently used by this application instance.

Item.Name: Persistent Storage Limit
Item.Description: Persistent storage limit currently enforced for this application instance.

Item.Name: Non-Persistent Storage Used
Item.Description: Non-persistent storage currently used by this application instance.

Item.Name: Non-Persistent Storage Limit
Item.Description: Non-persistent storage limit currently enforced for this application instance.

Item.Name: Thread Count
Item.Description: Current thread count associated with this application instance or representative runtime process, when the execution environment exposes that information.

Item.Name: Process Count
Item.Description: Current number of observed processes associated with this application instance, including directly associated child processes when exposed by the execution environment.

Item.Name: Last Platform Action
Item.Description:
Most recent platform action taken for this application instance.
0 = None 1 = Requested status 2 = Requested clean shutdown 3 = Stopped 4 = Killed or forcibly terminated 5 = Restarted 6 = Quarantined 7 = Unquarantined 8 = Purged local data 9 = Monitoring or runtime policy changed 10..127 = Reserved for future GEISA-defined platform actions 128..255 = Reserved for vendor-specific or operator-specific platform actions

Item.Name: Last Platform Action Time
Item.Description: Time at which the platform most recently took an action for this application instance. The value is encoded as LwM2M Time using Unix epoch seconds. The encoded value does not carry timezone metadata. Platform-local interpretation uses /3/0/15 Timezone, with fallback to /3/0/14 UTC Offset when Timezone is unavailable, unless a more specific ADM or EMS policy applies.

Item.Name: Request App Status
Item.Description: Request that the platform obtain or refresh application status. The platform MAY satisfy this by using local runtime state, by requesting status from the application, or both.

Item.Name: Request Clean Shutdown
Item.Description: Request that the platform ask the application to shut down cleanly. This operation is advisory and does not replace platform lifecycle enforcement through Software Management or platform execution control.

Item.Name: Monitoring Window
Item.Description: Platform-defined monitoring window in seconds used for average, peak, restart, crash, and watchdog-miss current-period metrics in this object instance.

Item.Name: Monitoring Period Start Time
Item.Description: Start time for the active monitoring period used by current-period counters and windowed metrics. The value is encoded as LwM2M Time using Unix epoch seconds. The encoded value does not carry timezone metadata. Platform-local interpretation uses /3/0/15 Timezone, with fallback to /3/0/14 UTC Offset when Timezone is unavailable, unless a more specific ADM or EMS policy applies.

Item.Name: Last Update Timestamp
Item.Description: Time at which the platform most recently refreshed or updated this AppMonitoring object snapshot for the application instance. The value is encoded as LwM2M Time using Unix epoch seconds. The encoded value does not carry timezone metadata. Platform-local interpretation uses /3/0/15 Timezone, with fallback to /3/0/14 UTC Offset when Timezone is unavailable, unless a more specific ADM or EMS policy applies.
